what: 6. Worked code examples per Report Card branch
title: GRASS methods companion: notation, calibration detail, and auxiliary diagnostics
text: small helper generates N x k rating matrix paper’s diagonal DGP stated seed, k, N, per-rater Se/Sp, target prevalence pi, logit-normal spread parameter F_sigma2:
code: library(grass)  simulate_panel <- function(seed, k, N, Se, Sp, pi, F_sigma2 = 0.25) {   stopifnot(length(Se) == k, length(Sp) == k)   set.seed(seed)   mu_logit <- qlogis(pi)   p_i <- plogis(rnorm(N, mu_logit, sqrt(F_sigma2)))   C   <- rbinom(N, 1L, p_i)   Y   <- matrix(0L, N, k)   for (j in seq_len(k)) {     Y[, j] <- rbinom(N, 1L, ifelse(C == 1L, Se[j], 1 - Sp[j]))   }   Y }

title: GRASS Report Card: a one-call workflow for binary rater reliability
text: entire workflow three lines: load package, pass N x k binary rating matrix, print result. block printed Report Card means something specific: sample describes study: rater count k, subject count N, observed positive rate pi_hat = mean(Y). primary coefficient line (e.g. PABAK = 0.41 -> 95th pct (decisive)) shows observed value, lands reference surface study’s (k, N, pi_hat), confidence qualifier (decisive / moderate / weak) reflecting local envelope width. percentile categorical score; four-way labeled band sits percentile reader. delta cross-coefficient percentile spread pp across agreement family (PABAK, AC1, Fleiss kappa; ICC excluded construction). caution divergent cuts size-α calibrated per (k, N) (delta_thresholds_lookup); modal applied design (k = 5, N = 200) pair (9.25, 10.75) pp. caution cut panel aligned primary coefficient safe cite; divergent cut panel- aggregate suppressed favor pairwise PABAK matrix per-rater pooled-reference Se/Sp.
code: library(grass) set.seed(2026)  # Tiny demo: 5 raters, 200 subjects, modest agreement. truth <- rbinom(200, 1, 0.30) Y <- sapply(1:5, function(j) {   ifelse(truth == 1, rbinom(200, 1, 0.85), rbinom(200, 1, 0.15)) })  grass_report(ratings = Y) #> GRASS Report Card #>  #>   sample      = 5 raters, N = 200, pi_hat = 0.34 #>   PABAK        = 0.47  ->  27th percentile  (decisive)  <- primary #>   AC1          = 0.52  ->  26th percentile #>   Fleiss kappa = 0.41  ->  31st percentile #>   ICC          = 0.53  ->  50th percentile  [distribution-sensitive] #>   delta       = 4.7 pp (aligned) #>   thresholds  = (9.25, 10.75) [calibrated at this (k, N)] #>  #>   Notes: #>     - Fitted-ICC F_key picked via glmer: mu_hat=-1.039, tau2_hat=3.669 -> F_key tau2=4.0000, mu=-0.847. #>     - Fitted-ICC reference (GLMM-gap corrected) at F_key=LN_mu=-0.847_tau2=4.0000, k=5, N=200 (family=logit_normal, M1=0.374). #>  #>   See `summary(...)` for full panel and CI details. #>   See `plot(...)` for a surface-position visualization.

Picking single number panel whose coefficients disagree surface position hide asymmetry. per-rater table appears. latent-class fit (Dawid-Skene EM k >= 3, Hui-Walter bounds k = 2) recovers rater’s sensitivity specificity bootstrap confidence intervals. recovered estimates pin panel’s structure: R1, R3, R5 land near (Se, Sp) = (0.95, 0.75), R2, R4 near (0.75, 0.95). bootstrap CIs wide latent-class likelihood becomes ridge-flat near balanced prevalence bootstrap resamples correct mode label-switched counterpart; point estimates pin local mode, without external orientation practitioner assert rating matrix alone raters - versus -callers.
code: set.seed(6) N <- 1000; k <- 5 # Alternating per-rater Se/Sp asymmetry: R1, R3, R5 favor sensitivity; # R2, R4 favor specificity. Subject-level positive probabilities are # logit-normal around balanced prevalence (the paper's divergent DGP). Se <- c(0.95, 0.75, 0.95, 0.75, 0.95) Sp <- c(0.75, 0.95, 0.75, 0.95, 0.75) p_i   <- plogis(rnorm(N, qlogis(0.50), sqrt(0.25))) truth <- rbinom(N, 1L, p_i) Y_div <- matrix(0L, N, k) for (j in seq_len(k)) {   Y_div[, j] <- rbinom(N, 1L, ifelse(truth == 1L, Se[j], 1 - Sp[j])) }  card_div <- grass_report(ratings = Y_div, bootstrap_B = 200) card_div #> GRASS Report Card #>  #>   sample      = 5 raters, N = 1000, pi_hat = 0.50 #>   PABAK         = 0.49  ->  40th percentile  <- primary #>   AC1           = 0.49  ->  58th percentile #>   Fleiss kappa  = 0.49  ->  41st percentile #>   ICC           = 0.62  ->  50th percentile  [distribution-sensitive] #>   panel-agg.  = suppressed (divergent) #>   delta       =  18 pp (divergent) #>   thresholds  = (8.50, 9.75) [calibrated at nearest grid cell (snapped)] #>  #>   pairwise PABAK / surface percentile (lower / upper): #>     R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     #>   R1    --     25%    79%    43%    98%  #>   R2    0.47   --     97%    90%    17%  #>   R3    0.51   0.41   --     97%    96%  #>   R4    0.48   0.52   0.42   --     63%  #>   R5    0.55   0.46   0.54   0.50   --   #>  #>   per-rater vs panel-majority of OTHER raters (pooled-reference): #>     R1   Se_tilde = 0.94  Sp_tilde = 0.77  (n_pos = 434, n_neg = 471, excl = 95) #>     R2   Se_tilde = 0.71  Sp_tilde = 0.95  (n_pos = 466, n_neg = 439, excl = 95) #>     R3   Se_tilde = 0.95  Sp_tilde = 0.71  (n_pos = 433, n_neg = 485, excl = 82) #>     R4   Se_tilde = 0.74  Sp_tilde = 0.95  (n_pos = 468, n_neg = 441, excl = 91) #>     R5   Se_tilde = 0.96  Sp_tilde = 0.78  (n_pos = 428, n_neg = 476, excl = 96) #>  #>   per-rater (latent-class fit; alongside pairwise): #>     R1   Se = 0.95  (0.23, 0.97)   Sp = 0.78  (0.06, 0.82) #>     R2   Se = 0.73  (0.05, 0.77)   Sp = 0.95  (0.29, 0.97) #>     R3   Se = 0.96  (0.29, 0.97)   Sp = 0.72  (0.05, 0.76) #>     R4   Se = 0.75  (0.05, 0.79)   Sp = 0.95  (0.26, 0.97) #>     R5   Se = 0.96  (0.22, 0.98)   Sp = 0.78  (0.04, 0.82) #>  #>   Notes: #>     - (k = 5, N = 1000) has no fully-calibrated threshold pair on the grid (or is off-grid); using nearest fully-calibrated cell (k = 5, N = 500). #>     - Fitted-ICC F_key picked via glmer: mu_hat=0.005, tau2_hat=5.278 -> F_key tau2=4.0000, mu=0.000. #>     - Fitted-ICC reference (GLMM-gap corrected) at F_key=LN_mu=+0.000_tau2=4.0000, k=5, N=1000 (family=logit_normal, M1=0.500). #>  #>   See `summary(...)` for full panel and CI details. #>   See `plot(...)` for a surface-position visualization.

what: Pairwise reliability under the divergent flag
title: GRASS Report Card: a one-call workflow for binary rater reliability
text: panel divergent framework’s recommended primary deliverable pairwise_agreement(): k x k matrix pairwise PABAK values, placed k = 2 reference surface pair’s observed marginal, plus per-rater behavior panel-majority k - 1 raters proxy reference. pairwise matrix exposes panel’s structure directly, pooled-reference per-rater table avoids latent-class identifiability problem (label-switching moot “reference” observable panel majority, latent class). Read per-rater table: R1, R3, R5 favoring sensitivity R2, R4 favoring specificity, pooled-reference estimates pin rater’s bias direction without ambiguity. R1’s Se_tilde high (calls true positives panel-majority rate) Sp_tilde lower (calls panel-majority negatives less reliably): -caller signature. R2 shows mirror: low Se_tilde, high Sp_tilde. latent-class fit’s wide bootstrap CIs paper’s divergent example live label-switched mode; pooled-reference per-rater table sidesteps ambiguity. pairwise PABAK matrix lower triangle shows actual pairwise agreements; upper triangle shows surface percentiles pair’s observed marginal. Note PABAK value can land different surface percentiles across pairs pair sees different pi_hat_ij; divergent flag exposing exactly prevalence--pair structure panel-aggregate summary averaged away.
code: pw <- pairwise_agreement(Y_div) pw #> GRASS Pairwise Reliability #>  #>   sample      = 5 raters, N = 1000, pi_hat = 0.50, tau2_hat = 0.122, axis = inter #>  #>   Pairwise PABAK (lower triangle = PABAK_ij; upper triangle = surface percentile): #>  #>    R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     #> R1     --   25%    79%    43%    98%  #> R2   0.47     --   97%    90%    17%  #> R3   0.51   0.41     --   97%    96%  #> R4   0.48   0.52   0.42     --   63%  #> R5   0.55   0.46   0.54   0.50     -- #>  #>   Per-rater behavior against pooled panel-majority: #>  #>  rater Se_tilde Sp_tilde n_pos n_neg n_excl #>     R1     0.94     0.77   434   471     95 #>     R2     0.71     0.95   466   439     95 #>     R3     0.95     0.71   433   485     82 #>     R4     0.74     0.95   468   441     91 #>     R5     0.96     0.78   428   476     96 #>   (Se_tilde, Sp_tilde are calls vs panel-majority of OTHER raters, #>    not against external truth. n_excl: subjects with tied majority, #>    excluded from the per-rater pool.)

code: position_on_surface(ratings = Y_sym, metric = "pabak") #> grass surface-position report (Target-2 reporting convention) #>   metric               : pabak #>   observed value       : 0.414 #>   design (pi_hat,k,N)  : (0.354, 5, 200) #>   q_hat (scaffold)     : 0.822 +/- 0.014 #>   surface percentile   : 0.954 #>   band probabilities   : #>     Poor       0.000 #>     Moderate   0.000 #>     Strong     1.000 <- modal #>     Excellent  0.000 #>   label                : Strong (decisive) #>   sampling method      : empirical check_asymmetry(ratings = Y_sym) #> GRASS panel asymmetry diagnostic #>  #>   delta_hat = 1.2 pp #>   flag      = aligned  (< 9.2) #>  #>   panel: #>     coefficient        observed   percentile (pp)   in delta_hat #>     pabak              0.41       95.4             yes #>     mean_ac1           0.46       94.2             yes #>     fleiss_kappa       0.36       94.7             yes #>     icc                0.46       50.0             no [distribution-sensitive] #>  #>   Surface caveats: #>     - Fitted-ICC F_key picked via glmer: mu_hat=-0.877, tau2_hat=2.801 -> F_key tau2=4.0000, mu=-0.847. #>     - Fitted-ICC reference (GLMM-gap corrected) at F_key=LN_mu=-0.847_tau2=4.0000, k=5, N=200 (family=logit_normal, M1=0.374). #>     - delta_hat is computed over the agreement family (PABAK, AC1, Fleiss, alpha). The intervals are #>         Hui-Walter (1980) inequality bounds, not point #>         estimates with sampling uncertainty.

what: 7. Surface-envelope clamp (an edge case worth knowing about)
title: GRASS Report Card: a one-call workflow for binary rater reliability
text: delta_hat measures cross-coefficient surface-percentile spread. one panel’s coefficients reports observed value falls outside achievable range reference surface study’s design (pi_hat, k, N), inversion q_hat clamps surface boundary percentile lands exactly 0 100. Since v0.2.2, check_asymmetry() grass_report() exclude clamped coefficients delta_hat whenever least two unclamped coefficients remain — otherwise single clamped coefficient inflate spread 100 pp fire false divergent flag. common trigger ICC N > 200. bundled fitted-ICC reference calibrated N {50, 200}; beyond , function falls back oracle ICC reference (closed-form, GLMM-gap correction), -predicts practitioner’s glmer-fit ICC clamps percentile 0: printed Report Card surfaces clamp two places: Notes section names coefficient excluded , divergent flag panel display marks clamped coefficient row [clamped - excluded delta]. panel data.frame returned object also carries clamped logical column programmatic inspection: trade-: coefficient calibrated reference user’s design contributes nothing diagnostic. benefit: delta_hat fire divergent, reflects genuine cross-coefficient surface-percentile disagreement rather envelope artifact. clamp cosmetic (three agreement coefficients align cleanly without ICC), Report Card’s verdict right one. clamping coincides real divergent panel (rare possible), Notes line clamped column let practitioner audit coefficients contributed diagnostic.
code: # A panel where ICC clamps but the three agreement coefficients align. set.seed(5) N <- 1000L; k <- 5L mu_logit <- qlogis(0.08) p_i <- plogis(rnorm(N, mu_logit, sqrt(0.25))) truth <- rbinom(N, 1, p_i) Y_clamp <- matrix(0L, N, k) for (j in seq_len(k)) {   Y_clamp[, j] <- rbinom(N, 1, ifelse(truth == 1, 0.85, 0.15)) } card_clamp <- grass_report(Y_clamp, bootstrap_B = 50) card_clamp #> GRASS Report Card #>  #>   sample      = 5 raters, N = 1000, pi_hat = 0.21 #>   PABAK        = 0.49  ->  46th percentile  (decisive)  <- primary #>   AC1          = 0.62  ->  46th percentile #>   Fleiss kappa = 0.23  ->  47th percentile #>   ICC          = 0.33  ->  50th percentile  [distribution-sensitive] #>   delta       =   1 pp (aligned) #>   thresholds  = (8.50, 9.75) [calibrated at nearest grid cell (snapped)] #>  #>   Notes: #>     - (k = 5, N = 1000) has no fully-calibrated threshold pair on the grid (or is off-grid); using nearest fully-calibrated cell (k = 5, N = 500). #>     - Fitted-ICC F_key picked via glmer: mu_hat=-1.716, tau2_hat=1.633 -> F_key tau2=1.0000, mu=-1.386. #>     - Fitted-ICC reference (GLMM-gap corrected) at F_key=LN_mu=-1.386_tau2=1.0000, k=5, N=1000 (family=logit_normal, M1=0.239). #>  #>   See `summary(...)` for full panel and CI details. #>   See `plot(...)` for a surface-position visualization. card_clamp$panel[, c("coefficient", "surface_percentile",                      "band", "clamped")] #>    coefficient surface_percentile   band clamped #> 1        pabak           46.26681 Strong   FALSE #> 2     mean_ac1           45.51257 Strong   FALSE #> 3 fleiss_kappa           46.51809 Strong   FALSE #> 4          icc           50.00000 Strong   FALSE

code: library(grass) set.seed(29)  # Five raters, 200 subjects, Se = Sp = 0.85, prevalence 0.30. truth <- rbinom(200, 1, 0.30) Y <- sapply(1:5, function(j) {   ifelse(truth == 1, rbinom(200, 1, 0.85), rbinom(200, 1, 0.15)) })  grass_report(ratings = Y) GRASS Report Card    sample      = 5 raters, N = 200, pi_hat = 0.35, tau2_hat = 0.083   PABAK  = 0.41  ->  95th pct (decisive)   delta       = 1.2 pp (aligned)    Notes:     - Fitted-ICC F_key picked via glmer: mu_hat=-0.877, tau2_hat=2.801         -> F_key tau2=4.0000, mu=-0.847.     - Fitted-ICC reference (GLMM-gap corrected) at F_key=LN_mu=-0.847_tau2=4.0000,         k=5, N=200 (family=logit_normal, M1=0.374).     - obs_value 0.4642 above achievable maximum (0.3381); q_hat clamped.     - Delta-method SE undefined: dE/dq near zero at q_hat. title: grass: Context-Conditioned Reporting for Binary Rater Reliability
text: Headline API. grass_report(ratings = Y) — single-call workflow returning four-field Report Card object (grass_card) print / summary / .data.frame / plot methods. Six plot() view types: surface (default), panel, thermometer, intervals, per_rater, diagnostic. Surface-position primitive. position_on_surface(ratings = Y, metric = ...) places one observed coefficient context-conditioned reference surface returns percentile decisive / moderate / weak confidence qualifier capturing local envelope width (modal-quartile sampling probability bootstrap perturbation (q_hat, pi_hat)). percentile categorical score; four-way labeled band interposed percentile reader. Closed-form references PABAK, AC1, Fleiss kappa; bundled fitted-ICC reference ICC across k {3, 5, 8, 15, 25} x N {30, 50, 75, 100, 200, 300, 500, 1000} 14-point q-grid. Cross-coefficient stability signal. check_asymmetry(ratings = Y) returns cross-coefficient surface-percentile spread delta_hat computed three-coefficient agreement family (PABAK, AC1, Fleiss kappa) plus aligned / caution / divergent flag per-(k, N) size-alpha calibrated thresholds. ICC reported panel [distribution-sensitive] marker enter delta_hat construction, reference surface depends full subject-prevalence distribution F rather (q, pi_+) alone. Divergent-flag recovery path. pairwise_agreement() returns pairwise PABAK matrix k = 2 reference surface plus per-rater pooled-reference (Se_tilde, Sp_tilde) panel-majority k - 1 raters. Auto-triggered grass_report() divergent flag. Latent-class fit. latent_class_fit() returns per-rater Dawid-Skene (Se_hat, Sp_hat) point estimates k >= 3 Hui-Walter bounds k = 2, bootstrap CIs. title: Cross-coefficient panel asymmetry diagnostic — check_asymmetry
text: delta_hat split-bias detector. fires raters tilt different directions across (Se, Sp) – e.g., one rater high-Se / low-Sp, another high-Sp / low-Se – four coefficients respond heterogeneous per-rater behavior differently end disagreeing panel sits surface. framework's failure mode, shared/uniform bias (every rater tilts direction, e.g., panel trained one protocol favoring specificity sensitivity), produces uniform percentile degradation across coefficients: small delta_hat, low minimum surface percentile. Shared bias detected panel's minimum surface percentile, delta_hat. divergent flag therefore identifies specific kind disagreement – cross-coefficient inversion heterogeneous rater behavior – routes user per-rater pairwise PABAK matrix pooled-reference (Se_tilde, Sp_tilde) diagnostic. coefficient positioned reference surface via position_on_surface(), yielding percentile [0, 100] pp. panel diagnostic delta_hat = max(percentile) - min(percentile), computed coefficients whose observed value sits within achievable range reference surface (see Surface-envelope clamp ).

title: Cross-coefficient panel asymmetry diagnostic — check_asymmetry
text: observed coefficient value falls outside achievable range reference surface study's design (pi_hat, k, N), inversion q_hat clamps boundary percentile lands exactly 0 100. Including clamped percentiles max-min delta_hat inflate panel-spread purely clamp, panel disagrees percentile. Since v0.2.1 function therefore excludes clamped coefficients delta_hat whenever least two unclamped coefficients remain. affected coefficients still shown returned panel data.frame clamped = TRUE surface percentile 0 100, note $notes names coefficients excluded. matters often ICC N > 200 (bundled fitted-ICC reference calibrated N = 200; beyond function falls back oracle reference, -predicts practitioner's GLMM-fit ICC therefore clamps). behavior guarantees divergent flag always reflects genuine cross-coefficient surface-percentile disagreement rather envelope artifact; trade-coefficient calibrated reference user's design contributes nothing diagnostic. fewer two unclamped coefficients remain, delta_hat falls back raw spread including clamped values note records fallback. Tier thresholds default c(9.25, 11.75) pp, Neyman-Pearson-motivated size-alpha cutoffs alpha = 0.05 alpha = 0.01 Sec.3.2 GRASS paper (see also App G operating characteristics). construction NP-optimal within test class threshold rules delta_hat; likelihood-ratio test joint distribution panel coefficients (delta_hat paired-margin difference, likelihood ratio). three flags : aligned (delta_hat < 9.25 pp): panel agrees surface position. single coefficient stable summary; primary coefficient (Table 2) carries headline. caution (9.25 <= delta_hat < 11.75 pp): panel mildly inconsistent. Report primary coefficient caution flag delta_hat value. divergent (delta_hat >= 11.75 pp): single coefficient stable summary. Use latent_class_fit() recover per-rater (Se, Sp) report instead. new check_asymmetry(ratings, ...) signature replaces v0.1.x check_asymmetry(se, sp, ...) per-rater signature. backward compatibility, calling check_asymmetry() se = ... sp = ... named arguments emits one-time deprecation hint routes call check_rater_asymmetry(). Supplying ratings = ... per-rater se = / sp = error.

title: Cross-coefficient panel asymmetry diagnostic — check_asymmetry
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) # Build a 5x200 symmetric panel -- should print as 'aligned'. Y <- matrix(rbinom(5 * 200, 1, 0.30), nrow = 200, ncol = 5) check_asymmetry(Y) #> GRASS panel asymmetry diagnostic #>  #>   delta_hat = 3.1 pp #>   flag      = aligned  (< 9.2) #>  #>   panel: #>     coefficient        observed   percentile (pp)   in delta_hat #>     pabak              0.16       47.5             yes #>     mean_ac1           0.27       46.2             yes #>     fleiss_kappa       0.00       49.3             yes #>     icc                0.00       0.0             no [distribution-sensitive] #>  #>   Surface caveats: #>     - Fitted-ICC F_key picked via glmer: mu_hat=-0.831, tau2_hat=0.011 -> F_key tau2=0.0625, mu=-0.847. #>     - Fitted-ICC reference (GLMM-gap corrected) at F_key=LN_mu=-0.847_tau2=0.0625, k=5, N=200 (family=logit_normal, M1=0.303). #>     - obs_value 0.0034 below achievable minimum (0.0834); q_hat clamped. #>     - Delta-method SE undefined: dE/dq near zero at q_hat. #>     - delta_hat is computed over the agreement family (PABAK, AC1, Fleiss, alpha). ICC is reported on the panel but does not enter delta_hat (v0.5.0 scope: ICC's reference surface depends on the full subject-prevalence distribution F and does not share the (q, pi_+) sufficient statistic the agreement family does). # }

text: se Numeric vector, one per rater. Per-rater sensitivity estimates [0, 1]. sp Numeric vector, one per rater. Per-rater specificity estimates [0, 1]. length se. rater Optional character vector rater labels, length se. Defaults "R1", "R2", ... . threshold_caution Boundary Tier 1 (ok) Tier 2 (caution). Default 0.05. threshold_unsafe Boundary Tier 2 (caution) Tier 3 (unsafe). Default 0.10. summary reduce per-rater gaps scalar delta_hat. title: Per-rater Se/Sp asymmetry diagnostic — check_rater_asymmetry
text: function appropriate user already per-rater Se/Sp estimates – typically Hui-Walter / Dawid-Skene latent-class fit (see latent_class_fit()), simulation known truth, reference-standard comparison. function called inside divergent branch new check_asymmetry() / grass_report() workflow per-rater (Se, Sp) become available. ratings-matrix input (N x k binary ratings available, per-rater Se/Sp), use check_asymmetry() instead – computes cross-coefficient percentile spread without requiring identifiability per-rater (Se, Sp). Tier thresholds follow three-tier architecture: Tier 1 – ok (delta_hat < 0.05): diagonal default. Report q_hat +/- SE plus EDR EMR_panel without per-rater disclosure. Tier 2 – caution (0.05 <= delta_hat < 0.10): diagonal + diagnostic. Report q_hat +/- SE caution flag delta_hat value. Tier 3 – unsafe (delta_hat >= 0.10): full latent-class. q_hat withheld primary; report per-rater (Se_j, Sp_j) via Hui-Walter / Dawid-Skene fit. PABAK's prevalence-flatness conditional Se = Sp; Tier 3 condition visibly broken. se sp identifiable single 2-rater binary table without external ground truth. Supply : () simulation known truth, (b) Hui-Walter / Dawid-Skene latent-class fit k >= 3 prevalence heterogeneity, (c) reference-standard comparison. Passing raw table() -diagonals se / sp wrong function detect error.

code: # Tier 1: symmetric raters -- safe to use q_hat as primary check_rater_asymmetry(se = c(0.86, 0.88, 0.84), sp = c(0.85, 0.87, 0.86)) #> grass rater asymmetry diagnostic (per-rater |Se - Sp|) #>   raters              : 3 #>   per-rater gaps |Se-Sp| #>     R1        Se = 0.860   Sp = 0.850   gap = 0.010 #>     R2        Se = 0.880   Sp = 0.870   gap = 0.010 #>     R3        Se = 0.840   Sp = 0.860   gap = 0.020 #>   delta_hat (max)    : 0.020 #>   thresholds           : caution = 0.050,  unsafe = 0.100 #>   tier                 : 1  (ok) #>   reporting guidance   : Diagonal default: report q_hat plus EDR / EMR_panel.  # Tier 2: one rater pressing the Se = Sp assumption check_rater_asymmetry(se = c(0.90, 0.88, 0.92), sp = c(0.82, 0.86, 0.85)) #> grass rater asymmetry diagnostic (per-rater |Se - Sp|) #>   raters              : 3 #>   per-rater gaps |Se-Sp| #>     R1        Se = 0.900   Sp = 0.820   gap = 0.080 #>     R2        Se = 0.880   Sp = 0.860   gap = 0.020 #>     R3        Se = 0.920   Sp = 0.850   gap = 0.070 #>   delta_hat (max)    : 0.080 #>   thresholds           : caution = 0.050,  unsafe = 0.100 #>   tier                 : 2  (caution) #>   reporting guidance   : Diagonal + diagnostic: report q_hat with caution flag and delta_hat.  # Tier 3: within-rater Se-favoring regime -- requires latent-class fit check_rater_asymmetry(se = c(0.95, 0.93, 0.94), sp = c(0.78, 0.80, 0.79)) #> grass rater asymmetry diagnostic (per-rater |Se - Sp|) #>   raters              : 3 #>   per-rater gaps |Se-Sp| #>     R1        Se = 0.950   Sp = 0.780   gap = 0.170 #>     R2        Se = 0.930   Sp = 0.800   gap = 0.130 #>     R3        Se = 0.940   Sp = 0.790   gap = 0.150 #>   delta_hat (max)    : 0.170 #>   thresholds           : caution = 0.050,  unsafe = 0.100 #>   tier                 : 3  (unsafe) #>   reporting guidance   : Full latent-class: withhold q_hat as primary; report per-rater (Se_j, Sp_j).

See ?normalize_ratings accepted shapes. axis One "inter" (default) "intra". title: Generate a GRASS Report Card from a rating matrix — grass_report
text: object class c("grass_card", "list") fields sample, coefficient, delta, panel, per_rater, surface, call, grass_version, timestamp, inputs, notes. See v0.2.0 paper-alignment design doc Sec.3.1 full structure. Percentile units. card$coefficient$surface_percentile card$panel$surface_percentile reported 0-100 scale (e.g., 46.3 means 46th percentile). underlying position_on_surface() returns percentile 0-1 fraction scale; grass_report() multiplies 100 match paper's prose convention. print format methods use ordinal notation ("46th percentile").

title: Generate a GRASS Report Card from a rating matrix — grass_report
text: body, order: Normalize ratings canonical N x k integer matrix Y derive pi_hat = mean(Y), k = ncol(Y), N = nrow(Y). Validate k >= 2; warn N < 10; note N < 30. Compute panel observed coefficients (compute_panel(), internal): k = 2, PABAK / AC1 / Cohen's kappa; k >= 3, PABAK / AC1 / Fleiss kappa / ICC. panel coefficient, position observed value DGP-calibrated reference surface via position_on_surface(). Pick primary coefficient via Table 2 (metric = "auto") accept user's override. Compute cross-coefficient percentile spread delta_hat (pp) via check_asymmetry() tier aligned /  caution / divergent. flag == "divergent": run latent_class_fit() (Dawid-Skene EM k >= 3; Hui-Walter bounds k = 2) attach per-rater (Se_j, Sp_j) table. Assemble grass_card S3 object.

title: The grass ecosystem roadmap — grass_roadmap
text: grass binary categorical-agreement submodule MEADOW framework. single headline entry point – grass_report() – takes N x k binary rating matrix returns grass_card: four-field Report Card carrying sample summary, primary coefficient surface-position percentile, four-band qualitative label, cross-coefficient asymmetry diagnostic delta_hat three-tier stability flag. full panel coefficients, percentiles, bootstrap CIs, reference-surface artifacts ride along object summary(), .data.frame(), plot() access.

title: The grass ecosystem roadmap — grass_roadmap
text: core idea animates framework: fixed interpretation bands (Landis-Koch 1977 descendants) mathematically invalid across prevalences, sample sizes, rater counts. Target-2 principle MEADOW context-conditioned reporting: percentile coefficient lands qualitative band label maps conditioned actual study (k, N, pi_hat), computed inverting calibrated reference surface rather read fixed table. grass ships binary submodule; FIELD planned ship alongside Paper 3 Target-2 contract variance-component reliability continuous outcomes.

title: The grass ecosystem roadmap — grass_roadmap
text: MEADOW umbrella; submodule covers one scale type. user-facing API submodule – single rating matrix , Report Card – user code works binary panel today work continuous panel FIELD ships. Earlier drafts roadmap referenced TURF separate MEADOW submodule nominal multi-rater agreement. retired: GRASS now covers binary multi-rater case (Fleiss kappa, Krippendorff alpha, observed ICC) directly, framework taxonomy collapses MEADOW = GRASS + FIELD.

title: The grass ecosystem roadmap — grass_roadmap
text: Target-2 contract across submodules: grass_report() – primary entry point; rating matrix , grass_card . grass_card carries four-field summary (sample, primary coefficient surface percentile, four-band label, delta_hat stability flag), full panel coefficients bootstrap CIs, reference-surface artifacts. position_on_surface() – granular access single coefficient's surface percentile, four-band label, qualifier. check_asymmetry() – granular access cross-coefficient delta_hat three-tier stability flag. latent_class_fit() – per-rater Se/Sp via Dawid-Skene EM (k >= 3) Hui-Walter bounds (k = 2), populated automatically divergent branch grass_report(). summary(), .data.frame(), plot() – layered access full underlying panel surface-position visualization.

title: The grass ecosystem roadmap — grass_roadmap
text: terms, used throughout package documentation printed Report Card, come merged GRASS binary-rater-reliability paper (Sec.Sec.3-4): context-conditioned reporting convention – principle percentile band reported coefficient must condition (k, N, pi_hat), fixed table. surface-position percentile – empirical percentile observed coefficient calibrated reference surface study's (k, N, pi_hat). four-band label – qualitative tier (Poor / Moderate / Strong / Excellent) mapped q_hat (operating-quality projection onto Se = Sp diagonal) via partition c(0.5, 0.625, 0.75, 0.875, 1.0). delta-hat (delta_hat) stability flag – cross-coefficient percentile spread (pp) three tiers aligned / caution / divergent NP-motivated size-alpha thresholds c(9.25, 11.75) (paper Sec.3.2, App G operating characteristics). divergent, band suppressed per-rater Se/Sp latent-class fit reported instead.

text: ratings N x k binary rating matrix. Rows subjects, columns raters, values 0, 1. Data.frame list--rater- columns inputs coerced. Requires N >= 10, k >= 2, NA, -constant columns. B Integer >= 0. Number nonparametric bootstrap replicates. B = 0 skips bootstrap returns EM point estimate (bounds k = 2) without CIs. method One "dawid_skene_em", "hui_walter", NULL (default). NULL, dispatches Hui-Walter k = 2 Dawid-Skene EM k >= 3. Supplying "dawid_skene_em" k = 2 errors – EM unidentified . max_iter Integer. EM iteration cap. Default 1000. tol Numeric. EM log-likelihood tolerance. Default 1e-6. seed Optional integer. Sets bootstrap RNG; EM deterministic. ... Reserved future extensions; currently ignored.

title: Latent-class fit: per-rater Sensitivity and Specificity from a binary rating matrix — latent_class_fit

B: integer; bootstrap replicates run. prevalence_hat: numeric estimated prevalence (NA k = 2). log_likelihood: numeric (NA k = 2).

title: Latent-class fit: per-rater Sensitivity and Specificity from a binary rating matrix — latent_class_fit
text: Two regimes, dispatched k: k >= 3: Dawid-Skene 1979 expectation-maximization. Latent binary class C_i 0, 1; conditional independence raters given C; parameters (pi, Se_j, Sp_j) j = 1..k. Initialized majority-vote consensus. Iterated log-likelihood tolerance tol max_iter. Hand-rolled base R, new package dependency. k == 2: Hui-Walter 1980 inequality bounds. Per-rater Se Sp point-identified single 2x2 table without external information (e.g., known-prevalence subgroup reference standard). return inequality bounds observed marginals support: rater j positive rate P_j, Se_j bounded P_j, Sp_j bounded 1 - P_j, 1. returned rows bound_only = TRUE se_hat = sp_hat = NA. documented limitation design, bug. Bootstrap CI (B > 0): nonparametric, subjects--replacement. k >= 3 EM refit bootstrap sample; k = 2 bounds recomputed bootstrap distribution bound midpoints. Reports empirical 2.5 / 97.5 percentiles per-rater Se_j Sp_j (bound midpoints k = 2).

title: Latent-class fit: per-rater Sensitivity and Specificity from a binary rating matrix — latent_class_fit
text: Dawid, . P. Skene, . M. (1979). Maximum likelihood estimation observer error-rates using EM algorithm. Applied Statistics, 28(1), 20–28. Hui, S. L. Walter, S. D. (1980). Estimating error rates diagnostic tests. Biometrics, 36(1), 167–171.

title: Latent-class fit: per-rater Sensitivity and Specificity from a binary rating matrix — latent_class_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) N <- 500; k <- 5 Se <- 0.90; Sp <- 0.85; pi <- 0.30 C <- rbinom(N, 1, pi) Y <- matrix(0L, N, k) for (j in seq_len(k))   Y[, j] <- rbinom(N, 1, ifelse(C == 1, Se, 1 - Sp)) fit <- latent_class_fit(Y, B = 200, seed = 1) print(fit) } # }

what: Krippendorff's alpha (nominal, binary, fully crossed) — obs_krippendorff_alpha
title: Krippendorff's alpha (nominal, binary, fully crossed) — obs_krippendorff_alpha
text: Fully-crossed design (m_i = k ). Hayes-Krippendorff coincidence- matrix reduction: o_{01} = 2 * sum_i r_i * (k - r_i) / (k - 1), n_0 = N*k - sum(r_i), n_1 = sum(r_i), n_total = N*k, alpha = 1 - (n_total - 1) * o_{01} / (2 * n_0 * n_1).

title: Pairwise reliability for a divergent panel — pairwise_agreement
text: object class c("grass_pairwise", "list") fields: pabak_matrix — \(k \times k\) symmetric numeric matrix; \([, j]\) \(\mathrm{PABAK}_{ij}\), diagonal 1. percentile_matrix — \(k \times k\) symmetric numeric (0–100); \([, j]\) surface percentile \(\mathrm{PABAK}_{ij}\) \(k = 2\) reference pair's observed marginal. Diagonal NA. marginal_matrix — \(k \times k\) symmetric numeric; \([, j]\) \(\hat\pi_{+, ij}\). Diagonal NA. band_matrix — \(k \times k\) character matrix; four-band label pairwise percentile. Diagonal NA. qualifier_matrix — \(k \times k\) character; decisive / moderate / weak per pairwise percentile. pooled_per_rater — data frame \(k\) rows, one per rater, columns rater, se_tilde, sp_tilde, n_pool_pos, n_pool_neg, n_pool_excluded (subjects tied panel majority). sample — list k, N, pi_hat, tau2_hat, axis. notes — character vector caveats, (e.g., undefined pooled-reference \(k = 2\)). call — matched call.

title: Pairwise reliability for a divergent panel — pairwise_agreement
text: function recommended primary deliverable grass_report() flags panel divergent; panel-aggregate coefficients longer summarize panel adequately, pairwise matrix exposes panel's structure directly (uniform inconsistency, sub-group clustering, single- rater outliers).

title: Pairwise reliability for a divergent panel — pairwise_agreement
code: set.seed(6) Se <- c(0.95, 0.75, 0.95, 0.75, 0.95) Sp <- c(0.75, 0.95, 0.75, 0.95, 0.75) truth <- rbinom(200, 1, 0.5) Y <- sapply(seq_along(Se),             function(j) ifelse(truth == 1,                                rbinom(200, 1, Se[j]),                                rbinom(200, 1, 1 - Sp[j]))) pw <- pairwise_agreement(Y) pw #> GRASS Pairwise Reliability #>  #>   sample      = 5 raters, N = 200, pi_hat = 0.51, tau2_hat = 0.117, axis = inter #>  #>   Pairwise PABAK (lower triangle = PABAK_ij; upper triangle = surface percentile): #>  #>    R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     #> R1     --   75%    31%    79%    50%  #> R2   0.40     --   38%    38%    69%  #> R3   0.61   0.47     --   69%    56%  #> R4   0.41   0.47   0.52     --   75%  #> R5   0.49   0.39   0.50   0.40     -- #>  #>   Per-rater behavior against pooled panel-majority: #>  #>  rater Se_tilde Sp_tilde n_pos n_neg n_excl #>     R1     0.98     0.72    88    93     19 #>     R2     0.71     0.95    95    83     22 #>     R3     0.99     0.77    89    94     17 #>     R4     0.75     0.90    97    86     17 #>     R5     0.90     0.74    91    91     18 #>   (Se_tilde, Sp_tilde are calls vs panel-majority of OTHER raters, #>    not against external truth. n_excl: subjects with tied majority, #>    excluded from the per-rater pool.)

title: Position an observed agreement coefficient on its DGP-calibrated surface — position_on_surface
text: Two methods implemented. Delta method (method = "delta", default): assumes q_hat approximately normal mean q_hat standard deviation se_q_hat obtained delta-method SE monotone metric inversion, computes P(q band_j) pnorm() band boundaries. summary-stats-fallback; require full simulation outputs. Empirical method (method = "empirical", default): uses 2,000-replication sampling distribution multirater_sim_v3 (symmetric-rater reference sim backing Paper 2) form empirical sampling distribution q_hat_rep nearest matched scenario cell (metric, F_key nearest pi_hat, k nearest, N nearest, q_true nearest q_hat). full per-rep data bundled (~300 MB); instead package ships precomputed 13-point empirical-quantile summary per-cell q_hat_rep vector. Band probabilities P(q_hat_rep band_j) recovered piecewise-linear interpolation empirical CDF. Callers want pass per-rep vector may supply surface_data$per_rep surface_data$q_grid_per_rep; paths continue override bundled lookup. design (pi_hat, k, N) falls outside simulated grid, nearest-neighbour clamping applied flagged notes.

title: grass 0.1.2
text: Round 5 — spec-dispatch architecture + reviewer 5/5 items.

title: grass 0.1.2
text: Spec-dispatch. grass_report() now takes spec = grass_spec_binary() primary argument. Metric families (binary today; ordinal, multirater, continuous planned) selected spec constructor rather family-specific function names. One package, one entry point, extensible future families without API churn. See ?grass_roadmap. Stub constructors grass_spec_ordinal(), grass_spec_multirater(), grass_spec_continuous() return valid placeholder specs users can write release-ready code; passing one grass_report() errors pointer ?grass_roadmap. Result objects now carry $spec slot holding spec used call. downstream verbs (print, plot, .data.frame, tidy, grass_methods, grass_format_report) dispatch result$spec$family.

title: grass 0.1.2
text: reference_level argument grass_spec_binary() accepts 0.70 / 0.80 / 0.85 / 0.90. band analytical Youden-J-optimal expected metric value Se = Sp = reference_level, closed form conditional independence two raters given true class. Previously "high" (= 0.85) "medium" (= 0.70) available. grass_methods(result) — GRRAS-compliant manuscript methods paragraph pre-filled study numbers. Supports format = c("markdown", "latex", "plain"). Templated future metric families slot without touching dispatcher. .parallel = TRUE grass_report_by() — backed future.apply::future_lapply() progressr::progressor() handler. Respects user’s active future::plan(). Optional dependencies; clean install-command error absent. ?grass_roadmap help page — framework taxonomy, ecosystem architecture, implemented today, planned. “grass ecosystem” vignette — one page one-package-many-families design.

title: grass 0.1.2
text: Reference curve estimand changed. Pre-0.1.2 shipped simulation-derived ROC classification threshold (classifier cutpoint separating rater scenarios different ground-truth quality bands). 0.1.2 ships expected metric value Se = Sp calibration point — cleaner analytical estimand closed-form interpretation. Numeric values differ reference_level = 0.85 vs pre-0.1.2 reference = "high" prevalences. Full Monte-Carlo regeneration empirical confidence bands deferred future release. Legacy reference = "high" / "medium" / "none" argument grass_report() deprecated (soft — emits one-time message). "high" maps reference_level = 0.85, "medium" 0.70, "none" NULL. grass_reference(quality = ...) deprecated; use grass_reference(reference_level = ...). Internal thresholds sysdata renamed reference_binary (long-form: prevalence × reference_level × metric). grass_reference_table() now returns long form default accepts optional reference_level filter. Package-internal ; user impact limited anyone previously calling grass:::thresholds.

title: grass 0.1.1
text: response = argument — stats-modelling alias rater_cols = (wide) rating = (long). Conflicting canonical + alias errors “disagree” message. grass_format_report(ci_width = TRUE) — appends "; kappa CI width = 0.26, wide" half-width cutpoints calibrated kappa: tight < 0.10, moderate < 0.20, wide >= 0.20. Default FALSE (opt-). Kappa Wilson-logit CIs run 2-2.5x wider component Se/Sp CIs N, cutpoints higher Se/Sp conventions diagnostic accuracy reporting. broom::tidy.grass_result() long-form — 9 rows (3 metrics × 3 quantities: estimate, reference, distance). Columns: metric, quantity, value, conf.low, conf.high, n, prevalence. conf.low/conf.high populated kappa estimate. value (estimate) generic column reference/distance rows read point estimates without CIs. Plots via ggplot(aes(metric, value, colour = quantity)) without reshape. grass_report_by(data, group, ...) — splits data group (bare symbol string), runs grass_report() per subset, returns tidy data.frame .cohort column carrying group value. Uses .data.frame(compact = TRUE) internally. .data.frame(r, compact = TRUE) argument (already earlier 0.1.1 draft; surface ). Wide-format error now adds soft id_col = hint exactly one column non-binary: “Column ‘xxx’ looks like identifier; try id_col = 'xxx'.” hint ambiguous (columns binary-looking). reset_grass_warnings() — clears -per-session message cache loops many studies consistently named columns re-emit fallthrough warning. ?grass_report now “Sample size caveats” section documenting two N thresholds (N < 10 warning, N < 30 print note).

title: grass 0.1.1
text: Default format now "wide" ("matrix") across grass_report(), grass_compute(), grass_prevalence(). Users passing data.frame longer need name format. grass_format_report() default now ascii = TRUE (FALSE). Safer Slack, Markdown, non-UTF-8 locales. Pass ascii = FALSE Unicode kappa RStudio console. validate_prevalence() now errors exactly 0 1 (degenerate cases reference defined). Values (0, 0.01) (0.99, 1) still warn clamp.
